---
title: "Current Priorities"
format: html
---

This page is intentionally compact. Use [Ideas and Future Directions](ideas.qmd)
as the living future-work scratchpad, [Experimental Findings](experiments/findings.qmd)
for evidence, and [Research Questions](questions.qmd) for the longer-lived
research framing.

Resolved migration notes, bug-fix logs, and older VIN v2 checklists were
removed from this page so it can stay useful as a current-state surface rather
than an archive dump.

## Immediate priorities

- Decide whether the project should stay inside the current ASE / EFM3D ecosystem or explicitly pivot to a different data or platform strategy.
- Decide how much effort should go into improving the current VINv3 scorer versus shifting attention toward multi-step planning and RL.
- Keep the Quarto docs aligned to the current codebase, `main.typ`, and the current slide deck.
- Clean up data handling and offline-cache boundaries so dataset, cache, and label-generation contracts stay easy to reason about.
- Clarify the next concrete path toward multi-step RL, including which counterfactual modalities can be synthesized and which cannot.

## Active technical risks

- Oracle supervision still depends on GT-mesh availability for only a small subset of ASE scenes.
- Multi-step planning is blocked by missing counterfactual modalities for intermediate views that are not part of the recorded ego-trajectory.
- Fine-detail reconstruction targets remain sensitive to downsampling choices for meshes and point clouds.

## Best next reading

- Current architecture: [aria_nbv Overview & Architecture](impl/aria_nbv_overview.qmd), [Repository Structure](repo_structure.qmd), [ASE Data Pipeline & Streamlit Usage](impl/data_pipeline_overview.qmd), [aria_nbv Implementation Overview](impl/oracle_rri_impl.qmd)
- Current model and training: [View Introspection Network (VIN) on EVL](impl/vin_nbv.qmd), [CORAL for Ordinal Regression](impl/coral_intergarion.qmd), [API Reference](../reference/index.qmd)
- Future work and open direction: [Ideas and Future Directions](ideas.qmd)
